description: A clean, minimalist designed headline card on a pale cream/off-white background, formatted like a news-article or press-release header. The large black sans-serif headline reads: 'U.S. Launches "Project Firewall" to Step Up H-1B Visa Enforcement'. Below it is a short coral/red horizontal divider rule, then a smaller black sub-headline: 'New Department of Labor initiative will tighten oversight of the H-1B program and increase investigations into employers'. There are no photos, seals, or other imagery in the cropped frame; the design relies entirely on typography. Propaganda/messaging technique: presents a DOL policy initiative in authoritative, news-like framing ('Project Firewall', 'Step Up... Enforcement') to signal a crackdown on H-1B visa abuse and protection of American workers. Target audience: the administration's base, American workers, and a general public receptive to immigration/visa enforcement messaging.
notable_text: U.S. Launches "Project Firewall" to Step Up H-1B Visa Enforcement; New Department of Labor initiative will tighten oversight of the H-1B program and increase investigations into employers
